product_overview: Web-based educational application for learning mathematical logic and step-by-step Isabelle proofs. Provides navigation between theory (wiki), exercise selection, and an interactive workspace with persistent theming and admin content tools.
core_goals: Provide an interactive, step-by-step workspace for solving Isabelle/Isar logic exercises
Organize exercises by bulletins/partials with a fast sidebar selection workflow
Offer a learnable wiki with syntax and rules examples for students
Support multiple resolution modes (declarative and applicative) at exercise start
Keep the app dependency-free (vanilla JS modules) and responsive across devices
Persist user preferences (theme) and provide admin tools for content management and GitHub integration

features.name: Navigation
features.description: Switch between landing page and the Logic module entry point.
user_flows: Open / -> Click Logic button to navigate to logic module -> See /logica/ loaded with exercises and module navigation
Open / -> Click Logic button to navigate to logic module -> See loading indicator -> See error banner 'Failed to load module'

features.name: Logic Module Exercise Selection (Sidebar)
features.description: Browse and select exercises from the sidebar menu organized by bulletins, parciales and convocatorias.
user_flows: Navigate to /logica/ -> Click sidebar item 'Boletín 1' to select exercises -> See exercise list load -> Click exercise title to open in workspace -> See workspace rendered with selected exercise
Navigate to /logica/ -> Click sidebar item 'Boletín 1' to select exercises -> See loading spinner -> See error message 'Exercise not found' or 'Failed to load exercises'

features.name: Wiki (Learning Module)
features.description: Interactive guide with Isabelle syntax, logical rules, and expandable example cards.
user_flows: Navigate to /logica/ -> Click Wiki tab in module navigation -> See wiki content loaded -> Click example card to expand -> See syntax example and code snippet
Navigate to /logica/ -> Click Wiki tab in module navigation -> See loading spinner -> See error 'Wiki content unavailable'

features.name: Logic Workspace
features.description: Render proof code, show step-by-step explanations, manage active hypotheses, and navigate proof steps.
user_flows: Navigate to /logica/ -> Click sidebar item to select exercise -> Click exercise to open workspace -> View rendered code -> View explanation panel showing current step -> Click Next button -> Observe hypotheses list update -> See progress bar advance

Navigate to /logica/ -> Click sidebar item to select exercise -> Click exercise to open workspace -> View rendered code -> Click Next button -> See error banner 'Failed to advance step' -> Workspace remains on same step

features.name: Modes of Resolution (Selection Modal)
features.description: When starting an exercise the user chooses between declarative (Isar) and applicative (tactic script) modes.
user_flows: Navigate to /logica/ -> Click sidebar item to select exercise -> Click Start Exercise -> See mode selection modal -> Click Modo Declarativa -> See workspace open with declarative view
Navigate to /logica/ -> Click sidebar item to select exercise -> Click Start Exercise -> See mode selection modal -> Click Modo Aplicativa -> See error 'Mode unavailable' or disabled option

features.name: Theme Toggle
features.description: Toggle between light and dark themes with persistence across reloads.
user_flows: Open / -> Click theme toggle button -> Observe UI switch to Dark mode -> Reload page -> Observe UI remains in Dark mode
Open / -> Click theme toggle button -> Observe UI switch to Dark mode -> Reload page -> Observe UI reverted to Light mode and see warning 'Failed to persist theme'

features.name: Admin Panel and Content Management
features.description: Hidden admin interface for creating/editing exercises, GitHub-based persistence and optional AI import tools for generating exercise JSON.
user_flows: Navigate to /logica/ -> Open hidden Admin Panel control -> Click Sign in with GitHub -> Complete GitHub auth -> Open content editor -> Create new exercise draft -> Click Save to repository -> See success confirmation
Navigate to /logica/ -> Open hidden Admin Panel control -> Click Sign in with GitHub -> See GitHub auth popup -> See error 'Authentication failed' -> Admin editor remains locked

routes.description: Landing page with navigation to Logic module
Logic module with exercises and theory

features.name: Navigation
features.description: Navigate between landing page and logic module
user_interactions: Click Logic button to navigate to logic module

features.name: Logic Module Exercise Selection
user_interactions: Click sidebar items to select exercises

features.name: Theme Toggle
features.description: Toggle between light and dark mode
